pitch.title: The Memory Mint
pitch.core_theme: The value of authentic human experience versus artificial happiness.
pitch.logline: In a dystopian future where human memories are traded as currency, a desperate cop must expose a counterfeiting ring injecting fake, euphoric memories into the populace before society entirely loses its grip on reality.
pitch.synopsis: Act I: Detective Kael investigates a series of bizarre suicides linked to individuals with impossibly perfect memories. He discovers the existence of synthetics, which are illegal counterfeit memories. Act II: Kael goes undercover into the memory black market, partnering with a rogue memory-weaver. They uncover a massive conspiracy: the government is the one minting fake memories to keep citizens docile. Act III: Kael must choose between keeping his own painful memories of his deceased family or accepting a synthetic reality. He ultimately decides to destroy the central mint and wake the city up to their painful but real lives.

stage2_outline.title: The Memory Mint
stage2_outline.logline: In a dystopian future where human memories are traded as currency, a desperate cop must expose a counterfeiting ring injecting fake, euphoric memories into the populace before society entirely loses its grip on reality.

beats.description: Detective Kael numbs the pain of his deceased family in a gritty dystopian city where authentic memories are traded as hard currency.
Kael investigates a bizarre suicide and extracts the victim's final memory, discovering an impossibly euphoric, synthetic experience.
Kael links multiple suicides to these illegal synthetic memories, realizing a massive counterfeiting ring is flooding the streets.
When corrupt superiors order him to close the case, Kael defies them and plunges into the perilous black market to find the source.
Kael navigates the dangerous memory-trading underworld and apprehends a cynical but brilliant rogue memory-weaver named Elara.
Elara agrees to help Kael track the synthetic origin in exchange for her freedom, forcing an uneasy partnership.
Kael and Elara break into a heavily guarded distribution hub to trace the counterfeit memory codes back to their origin point.
They uncover a massive conspiracy. The synthetics are not from a cartel, but minted by the government to keep the impoverished populace docile.
Government forces, alerted to the breach, launch a ruthless manhunt, forcing Kael and Elara into a desperate retreat.
Their safehouses are compromised, and they barely survive a brutal ambush by elite memory-wiping enforcement squads.
Kael is captured by the authorities and Elara is presumed dead, leaving the government's secret entirely safe.
The government offers Kael a permanent synthetic memory where his family never died. He rejects the artificial happiness, escapes custody, and reunites with a surviving Elara.
Accepting his painful reality, Kael and Elara arm themselves and devise a desperate, suicide-mission plan to infiltrate the central Memory Mint.
They storm the Mint, fighting through waves of heavily armed security forces to reach the main server broadcasting the synthetic euphoria.
Kael engages in a brutal final showdown with the Mint Director, ultimately destroying the central server and halting the synthetic broadcast.
The city wakes up to its painful, unvarnished reality. Kael looks out over the chaotic but authentic world, finally at peace with his grief.
